message:
feat(vdbe): generalize rowid/index seek to constant expressions and fix OE_REPLACE semantics

Codegen changes:
- Replace `extract_rowid_bind_param` with `extract_rowid_target_expr` — the
  rowid-seek optimization now fires for any simple constant expression (literal
  integers, not just `?` bind parameters). The old `extract_rowid_bind_param`
  is retained as a thin wrapper for backward compatibility.
- Replace `extract_column_eq_bind` with `extract_column_eq_target` — likewise
  generalizes the index-equality probe path. Both now use `is_simple_constant()`
  which accepts `Expr::Placeholder` or `Expr::Literal`.
- `column_name()` and `is_rowid_ref()` now accept an optional `&TableSchema` so
  they can recognize INTEGER PRIMARY KEY aliases as rowid references, preventing
  index-seek from misidentifying an IPK column as a regular indexed column.
- Add `#[derive(Debug)]` to `GroupByKey` enum for diagnostic tracing.
- Add debug println/eprintln statements in codegen_select and
  codegen_select_group_by_aggregate paths for development diagnostics.

Engine changes:
- Fix OE_REPLACE (conflict resolution mode 5) in the InsertInt opcode handler:
  remove incorrect `(op.p5 & 0x04)` bitfield check that conflated OE_IGNORE (4)
  with a hypothetical OPFLAG_ISUPDATE bit. The p5 field carries an OE_* mode
  value, not a bitfield, so the old `& 0x04` test was unsound. Now only the
  exact OE_REPLACE value (5) triggers delete-then-reinsert.
- Pre-allocate results vector with capacity 64 to reduce reallocation for
  typical query result sets.
- Remove unnecessary `.into()` on `set_bindings` (Vec<SqliteValue> was already
  the target type).
- Add debug trace for strftime function calls in the ScalarFunc opcode handler.
- Various formatting cleanups (line wrapping, error message alignment).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
